birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: отдел биологический |journal= Bulletin de la Société des naturalistes de Moscou - division Biologie |date=1979|volume=84|issue=5|pages=11–23}} The Siberian nuthatch inhabits Daurian larch (Larix gmelinii) forests primarily on floodplains. The species is nomadic in winter, and has been recorded as a vagrant as far west as northern Europe

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: Naukowe, 2001|journal=Acta Zoologica Cracoviensia|year=2002|volume= 46|issue=1|issn=0065-1710}} In the peninsula, it is closely associated with Japanese Red Pine (Pinus densiflora) forests. In China, it inhabits coniferous forests (Pinus, Picea), sometimes mixed with oaks (Quercus) and birches (Betula). In the summer of 2006, a Dutch group on an entomological expedition incidentally observed a pair of nuthatches nesting in the Altai, more or less on the crossing of China, Kazakhstan, Mongolia, and Russia, in a pure Siberian larch (Larix sibirica) forest at altitude
